name: Akasaka (Minato)
description: Minato is a special ward of Tokyo, Japan. It is also called Minato City in English.

name: Akihabara (Chiyoda)
description: Akihabara is a neighborhood in the Chiyoda ward of Tokyo, Japan, generally considered to be the area surrounding Akihabara Station. This area is part of the Sotokanda (外神田) and Kanda-Sakumachō districts of Chiyoda. There is an administrative district called Akihabara, located north of Akihabara Electric Town surrounding Akihabara Neribei Park.

name: Asakusa (Taito)
description: Asakusa is a district in Taitō, Tokyo, Japan. It is known for Sensō-ji, a Buddhist temple dedicated to the bodhisattva Kannon. There are several other temples in Asakusa, as well as various festivals, such as Sanja Matsuri.

name: Ashigara
description: Ashigara is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Atami
description: Atami is a scenic destination in the Shizuoka region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Boso Peninsula
description: Boso Peninsula is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Chiba City
description: Chiba City is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Chichibu
description: Chichibu is a scenic destination in the Saitama region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Chofu
description: Leafy suburban Tokyo town famous for Jindaiji Temple, hand-made Soba noodles, and GeGeGe no Kitaro anime heritage.
notes: Jindaiji is Tokyo second oldest temple after Asakusa Sensoji.

name: Choshi
description: Easternmost coastal cape of the Kanto region, renowned for first sunrises, Inubosaki Lighthouse, and fresh seafood markets.
notes: Inubosaki is famous for being the first place on mainland Japan to see the New Year sunrise.

name: Tokyo Disneyland
description: Tokyo Disneyland is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: DisneySea
description: DisneySea is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Edogawa

name: Fuji Five Lakes
description: Fuji Five Lakes is a scenic destination in the Yamanashi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Fujinomiya
description: Fujinomiya is a city located in central Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan. As of 1 July 2019, the city had an estimated population of 132,507 in 56,655 households, and a population density of 340 persons per km2. The total area of the city is 388.99 square kilometres (150.19 sq mi).

name: Fukui
description: Fukui is a scenic destination in the Fukui region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Gala Yuzawa
description: Gala Yuzawa is a scenic destination in the Niigata region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Ghibli Museum
description: Ghibli Museum is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Ginza
description: Ginza is a district of Chūō, Tokyo, located south of Yaesu and Kyōbashi, west of Tsukiji, east of Yūrakuchō and Uchisaiwaichō, and north of Shinbashi. It is a popular upscale shopping area of Tokyo, with numerous internationally renowned department stores, boutiques, restaurants and coffeehouses located in the vicinity.

name: Gujo Hachiman
description: Gujo Hachiman is a scenic destination in the Gifu region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Hachioji
description: Historic castle town and mountain gateway at the base of Mt. Takao, offering scenic nature trails and Yakiniku food culture.
notes: Direct Keio line express trains from Shinjuku.

name: Hakkeijima Sea Paradise
description: Hakkeijima Sea Paradise is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Hakone
description: Hakone is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Harry Potter Studio
description: Harry Potter Studio is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Hitachi Seaside Park
description: Hitachi Seaside Park is a scenic destination in the Ibaraki region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Ikaho Onsen
description: Atmospheric thermal spring village built around a 365-step stone staircase lined with traditional ryokans, footbaths, and souvenir shops.
parking: Available at entrance of stone steps
notes: Nearby Mizusawa Udon is one of Japan top three udon noodle traditions.

name: Kamakura
description: Kamakura , officially Kamakura City , is a city of Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan. It is located in the Kantō region on the island of Honshu. The city has an estimated population of 172,929 and a population density of 4,359 people per km2 over the total area of 39.67 km2 (15.32 sq mi). Kamakura was designated as a city on 3 November 1939.

name: Kamikochi
description: Kamikochi is a scenic destination in the Nagano region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Kanazawa
description: Kanazawa is the capital of Ishikawa Prefecture in central Japan. As of 1 January 2018, the city had an estimated population of 466,029 in 203,271 households, and a population density of 990 persons per km2. The total area of the city was 468.64 square kilometres (180.94 sq mi).

name: Karuizawa
description: Karuizawa is a scenic destination in the Nagano region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

description: Kinugawa Onsen is a scenic destination in the Tochigi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Kiso Valley

name: Lake Sagami
description: Lake Sagami is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Machida
description: Vibrant shopping and commercial hub in western Tokyo bordering Kanagawa, famous for Serigaya Park and Squirrel Garden.
notes: Easy train access via Odakyu or JR Yokohama Line.

name: Matsumoto
description: Matsumoto is a scenic destination in the Nagano region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Meguro
description: Meguro is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Mito
description: Mito is a scenic destination in the Ibaraki region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Mother Farm (Futtsu)
description: Sprawling 250-hectare mountain farm overlooking Tokyo Bay with alpaca feeding, sheep shearing shows, and flower fields.
notes: Famous for fresh dairy ice cream and seasonal petunia and nemophila blooms.

name: Mount Fuji
description: Mount Fuji is an active stratovolcano located on the Japanese island of Honshu, with a summit elevation of 3,776.24 m. It is the highest mountain in Japan, the second-highest volcano on any Asian island, and the seventh-highest peak of an island on Earth. Mount Fuji last erupted from 1707 to 1708.

name: Nagano & Snow Monkeys
description: Nagano & Snow Monkeys is a scenic destination in the Nagano region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Nagatoro
description: Scenic river valley famous for traditional wooden boat rafting down the Arakawa, Iwadatami rock formations, and autumn foliage.
reservation: Recommended for boat rafting in peak season
notes: Famous for natural shaved ice kakigori using frozen winter river ice.

name: Nagoya
description: Nagoya is a scenic destination in the Aichi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Narita
description: Narita is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Nikko
description: Nikko is a scenic destination in the Tochigi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Noto Peninsula
description: Noto Peninsula is a scenic destination in the Ishikawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Odaiba (Minato)
description: Odaiba (Minato) is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Odawara & Manazuru
description: Odawara & Manazuru is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Okuhida
description: Okuhida is a scenic destination in the Gifu region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Okutama
description: Tokyos wildest mountain escape featuring Lake Okutama, dramatic limestone caves, suspension bridges, and riverside hot springs.
notes: Moegi no Yu hot spring near Okutama station is perfect after a hike.

name: Ome
description: Nostalgic Showa-era movie poster town in western Tokyo surrounded by Mitake Gorge, river rafting, and mountain shrines.
notes: Visit Ozawa Brewery (Sawanoi) for sake tasting along the river.

name: Omiya Railway Museum
description: Ōmiya is one of ten wards of the city of Saitama, in Saitama Prefecture, Japan, and is located in the northeastern part of the city. As of 1 March 2021, the ward had an estimated population of 119,298 and a population density of 9,300 persons per km2. Its total area was 12.80 square kilometres (4.94 sq mi). Although Urawa-ku is the governmental center of Saitama City, Ōmiya-ku is the most active commercial and business centre in both Saitama City and Saitama Prefecture thanks to its transport infrastructure, especially railways connected at Ōmiya Station.

name: Oze National Park
description: Oze National Park , is an area consisting of open greenland in Fukushima, Tochigi, Gunma and Niigata Prefectures in Japan. The park is 372 km2 in area and is the 29th national park in Japan.

name: Sado Island
description: Sado Island is an island located in the eastern part of the Sea of Japan, under the jurisdiction of Sado City, Niigata Prefecture, Japan, with a coastline of 262.7 kilometres (163.2 mi). In October 2017, Sado Island had a population of 55,212 people. Sado Island covers an area of 854.76 km2 (330.02 sq mi), and is the sixth largest island in Japan, excluding the disputed Southern Kurils. The shortest distance between Sado Island and Honshu is 32 km (20 mi). The highest peak on Sado Island is Mount Kinpoku, with an elevation of 1,172 m (3,845 ft).

name: Saitama City
description: Saitama is the capital and largest city of Saitama Prefecture, Japan. Its area incorporates the former cities of Urawa, Ōmiya, Yono and Iwatsuki. It is a city designated by government ordinance. As of 1 February 2021, the city had an estimated population of 1,324,854, and a population density of 6,093 people per km2. Its total area is 217.43 square kilometres (83.95 sq mi).

name: Shibuya
description: Shibuya is a special ward in Tokyo, Japan. A major commercial center, Shibuya houses two of the busiest railway stations in the world, Shibuya Station and Shinjuku Station.

name: Shinjuku
description: Shinjuku , officially called Shinjuku City, is one of the 23 special wards of Tokyo.

name: Shizuoka City
description: Shizuoka is the capital city of Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan, and the prefecture's second-largest city in both population and area. It has been populated since prehistoric times. As of 1 September 2023, the city had an estimated population of 677,867 in 106,087 households, and a population density of 480 inhabitants per square kilometre (1,200/sq mi).

name: Lake Suwa
description: High-altitude lake surrounded by mountains, famous for Suwa Taisha shrine complex, fireworks, lakeside hot springs, and winter ice phenomena.
notes: Tateishi Park offers breathtaking sunset views inspired by anime scenery.

name: Tachikawa
description: Major Tama region gateway featuring the massive Showa Kinen Park, seasonal flower festivals, and modern dining quarters.
notes: Showa Kinen Park is famous for autumn ginkgo and spring cherry blossoms.

name: Takaoka
description: Takaoka is a scenic destination in the Toyama region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Takayama & Shirakawa-go
description: Takayama & Shirakawa-go is a scenic destination in the Gifu region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: teamLab Planets
description: teamLab Planets is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route
description: Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route is a scenic destination in the Toyama region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Mt. Tsukuba
description: Mt. Tsukuba is a scenic destination in the Ibaraki region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Ueno (Taito)
description: Ueno (Taito) is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Ushiku Daibutsu
description: Ushiku Daibutsu is a scenic destination in the Ibaraki region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Utsunomiya & Oya
description: Utsunomiya & Oya is a scenic destination in the Tochigi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Yokohama
description: Yokohama is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

name: Yomiuriland
description: Yomiuriland is an amusement park in Inagi, Tokyo and Tama-ku, Kawasaki, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan that first opened on 19 March 1964. It is situated on hillsides, and features rides such as roller coasters and water flumes. It is home to Yomiuri Giants Stadium, one of the training fields for the Yomiuri Giants baseball team, and was the primary training ground before Tokyo Dome was completed. It is operated and run by Yomiuriland Co., Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of The Yomiuri Shimbun Holdings.

name: Zushi & Yokosuka
description: Zushi & Yokosuka is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
